summary.notes: CSS/JS architecture is well-organized after Phase 3. One CSS deduplication performed: @keyframes inv-spin was defined 4 times across 3 files — consolidated to a single canonical definition in base.css. Dead code referencing deleted invicta-collection-filters.liquid was also cleaned up.

css_analysis.conclusion: No CSS files were removed or merged. One deduplication performed: @keyframes inv-spin consolidated from 4 definitions (3 files) to 1 canonical definition in base.css. No override conflicts or redundant files found.

findings.finding: component-* CSS files used by invicta snippets use inv-* class prefixes despite the component-* filename
findings.action: No change. These are invicta CSS with legacy filenames. Renaming is cosmetic and risks breaking Shopify asset CDN cache. The naming convention is documented in ARCHITECTURE.md.
findings.reason: Per constraints: mass renaming has a terrible risk-to-reward ratio in a Shopify theme.

findings.finding: component-cart.css and invicta-cart.css have 9 overlapping CSS class selectors
findings.action: No change. Both files are required. component-cart.css provides base Dawn cart styles, invicta-cart.css adds trade-specific customizations using more specific selectors (e.g., .inv-cart-footer-grid__checkout .cart__checkout-button). This is intentional cascade-based architecture.
findings.reason: Removing either file would break cart styling. The invicta file uses higher-specificity selectors to override selectively, not wholesale.

findings.action: No change. Zero overlapping classes. component-predictive-search.css styles Dawn's .predictive-search classes (used on search results page via main-search.liquid). invicta-predictive-search.css styles .inv-search-* classes (used in header inline search via predictive-search-inline.liquid). They are complementary implementations.
findings.reason: Different class namespaces, different features, both in active use.

findings.finding: Several CSS files are small (under 30 lines)
findings.action: No change. Each small file is loaded on-demand by exactly one section or snippet. Merging them would force unnecessary CSS onto pages that don't need it, hurting performance. On-demand loading is the correct pattern for Shopify OS 2.0.
findings.reason: File size alone is not a reason to merge in a section-driven architecture. The overhead of an extra HTTP request is negligible compared to unused CSS bloat.

findings.finding: Global CSS loading in theme.liquid is well-structured
findings.action: No change. Loading strategy is already optimized with appropriate use of lazy loading (print→all pattern), media queries, and conditional loading.
findings.reason: Current global loading is already minimal and well-optimized.

findings.finding: @keyframes inv-spin was defined 4 times across 3 CSS files
findings.action: FIXED. Removed all 4 duplicate definitions. Added single canonical @keyframes inv-spin to assets/base.css (loaded synchronously on every page, before any lazy-loaded CSS that references the animation).
findings.reason: All definitions were functionally identical (rotate 0→360deg). base.css is the foundation file loaded first, making the animation available everywhere. No animation references needed updating — only the @keyframes declarations were deduplicated.

js_analysis.conclusion: No JS files were removed or merged. All JS files serve distinct purposes with no duplication.

findings.finding: Cart-related JS files appear similar but are complementary

files.cart.js: Dawn cart page form submission handler (296 lines)
files.invicta-cart-api.js: Shared add-to-cart utility with deduplication (163 lines)
files.invicta-cart-handler.js: Handles cart UI updates after add-to-cart (88 lines)

findings.action: No change. Each has a distinct responsibility: cart.js handles the full cart page, cart-drawer.js handles the drawer UI, invicta-cart-api.js centralizes fetch calls, invicta-cart-handler.js manages cart count/drawer updates.
findings.reason: JavaScript has runtime dependencies. These files are intentionally separated for different loading contexts.

findings.finding: Predictive search has two JS implementations

files.predictive-search.js: Dawn predictive search for search results page (334 lines)

findings.action: No change. Different implementations for different UI contexts. predictive-search.js is loaded conditionally in theme.liquid. invicta-predictive-search.js is loaded by the predictive-search-inline.liquid snippet.
findings.reason: Different features, different DOM targets, different loading contexts.

global_js: invicta-cart-handler.js (88 lines — cart UI handler, via snippet)
findings.action: No change. Global scripts are all small utility files or core components needed on every page. Larger feature scripts are conditionally loaded.

svg_audit.note: Unreferenced SVGs may be used in Shopify admin content (metafields, rich text, page content) that is not visible in theme files. Do NOT auto-delete — flag for manual review by theme owner.
svg_audit.brand_logo_loading_mechanism: Brand logos are loaded dynamically in snippets/invicta-brand-pill.liquid and snippets/invicta-brand-hero.liquid using the pattern: brand_key | append: '.svg' | asset_url. The brand key is derived from the product vendor name or a hardcoded brand database.

orphaned_brand_logos.note: Not in the brand database and not referenced anywhere in theme files. Likely orphaned.
Brand DB uses key 'teng' not 'tengtools', so the dynamic loader would look for 'teng.svg', not 'tengtools.svg'. This SVG filename does not match the dynamic loading pattern. Likely orphaned.

identical_groups.differences: Settings only — brand name, images, content, colours. All use identical section structure.
identical_groups.note: page.brand.json is NEARLY identical but uses slightly fewer sections (no brand-faq, no brand-schema, adds breadcrumb-simple). This is the generic brand template.

identical_groups.differences: Settings only — category data, icons, collection links.
Single section only. These 3 lack the invicta-recently-viewed section that the other 15 category pages have — this may be an inconsistency rather than intentional.
Structurally identical — both use only main-page. page.category-landing.json appears redundant but may be used by specific admin pages.
page.kent-hub uses local-hero + local-hub. page.local-seo uses local-hero + local-content.
Each uses a single unique main-* section. Standard Shopify pattern.

observations: page.category-landing.json is structurally identical to page.json — both use only main-page. The owner should check if any admin pages use it; if not, it could be removed.

page.brand.json (generic brand template) has drifted from the 8 specific brand templates: it is missing brand-faq and brand-schema sections, and has breadcrumb-simple which the others lack. Consider aligning it if it is still in active use.
3 category pages (accessories-consumables, shop-by-brand, workwear-safety) lack the invicta-recently-viewed section that the other 15 category pages have. This may be an oversight.
template_analysis.recommendation: The 8 brand-specific templates and 18 category templates are structurally identical with different settings. The theme owner could consider whether some admin pages should be migrated to use a single template with dynamic content, but this is a Shopify admin decision, not a theme file change.

conventions.main-*: Shopify core sections (cart, account, search, blog, etc.). Standard Dawn pattern. Keep as-is.
conventions.brand-*: Brand landing page sections. Coherent group used by brand templates. Keep as-is.
conventions.invicta-*: Custom Invicta business logic. The standard for new files. CSS uses inv-* class prefix.
conventions.component-*: Shared component CSS/JS. Some are Dawn originals, some are Invicta CSS with legacy names. Keep as-is — renaming risks cache/reference issues.
conventions.section-*: Section-specific CSS. Loaded on-demand by the corresponding section.

changes.action: Removed stale comment and dead JS code referencing invicta-collection-filters.liquid (deleted in Phase 3)
changes.reason: invicta-collection-filters.liquid was deleted in Phase 3. The product grid had a stale comment mentioning it and two dead JS code blocks that checked for a DOM element (getElementById('invicta-collection-filters')) that will never exist. Code was safely guarded with null checks so it wasn't breaking, but it was dead weight.

changes.action: Consolidated @keyframes inv-spin to single canonical definition in base.css
changes.reason: @keyframes inv-spin was defined 4 times across 3 files (2 in invicta-cx-improvements.css, 1 in mobile.css, 1 in invicta-predictive-search.css). All definitions were identical (rotate 0→360deg). Consolidated to base.css which loads synchronously on every page, ensuring the animation is available before any lazy-loaded CSS that references it. No animation references were changed — only the @keyframes definitions were deduplicated.
